message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="blue" summary="Approved with one blocking fix: delete aria-label">
Accessibility review for Task #15 (Inbox Capture).

**APPROVED WITH ONE BLOCKING FIX.**

**Blocking: Delete button needs item-specific accessible name**

In `templates/inbox_item.html` line 11, the delete button just says "Delete":
```html
<button type="submit" class="inbox-item__delete-button">Delete</button>
```

Screen reader users tabbing through multiple inbox items will hear "Delete", "Delete", "Delete" with no way to tell which item each button acts on. This violates WCAG SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) and SC 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels). Fix:
```html
<button type="submit" class="inbox-item__delete-button" aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }}">Delete</button>
```

This matches the pattern already used in the todo list (US-7).

**Non-blocking note: discarded error message for too-long titles**

In `src/routes/inbox.rs` line 135, the `user_facing` error string for titles exceeding 300 chars is computed then discarded (`let _ = user_facing;`). The template has no error display. In practice, `maxlength="300"` on the input prevents this for most users, so this is non-blocking -- but it should be wired up eventually for the no-JS case where `maxlength` may not be enforced by older clients.

**Everything else looks solid:**
- Semantic `<ul role="list">` for the inbox list
- Visible `<label>` properly associated via `for`/`id`
- Quick capture correctly hidden on inbox page (no duplicate inputs)
- `aria-label="Quick capture"` on the header form
- Inbox badge with `aria-label="(N items to process)"` for screen readers
- Empty state with descriptive text and links
- `hidden` attribute on empty list removes it from a11y tree
- "Captured to inbox" / "Inbox item deleted" aria-live announcements
- Focus returns to input after capture
- axe-core scanning in e2e for both empty and populated states
- Delete button correctly sets explicit `color` to avoid invisible text

One fix, then I'm at full approval.
</teammate-message>
